title: The Wolf and the Lamb
lines: A Wolf saw a Lamb drinking at a stream.
The Wolf wanted to eat it, and he cast about for some excuse to quarrel with it.
"Why are you fouling the water from which I drink?" he asked angrily.
"I am not fouling it," replied the Lamb humbly; "the water flows from you to me, so I could not foul it."
"Besides, I have only just come to the water."
Realizing that he could not pick a quarrel with the Lamb, the Wolf cried out: "Your father once insulted me!"
"I was not even born then," protested the Lamb.
"Well, you are fouling the water now," growled the Wolf, and without another word he seized the poor Lamb and carried her off.
moral: The wicked will always find an excuse for doing what they want to do.
